---
title: "Assignment_04"
author: "Sharon Tsukernik"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Loading libraries
library(Seurat)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Mm.eg.db)   # mouse genes
library(devtools)
library(SingleR)
library(celldex)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(SingleCellExperiment)

```


```{r}
# Loading and inspecting data

seurat <- readRDS("seurat_ass4.rds")

seurat
head(seurat@meta.data)
```


```{r}
# Quality Control

# Mitochondrial ratio
seurat[["percent.mt"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(seurat, pattern = "^mt-")

# Visualizing QC metrics as a violin before filtering
VlnPlot(
  seurat,
  features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),
  group.by = "orig.ident",
  ncol = 3,
  pt.size = 0
) 

# Scatter plot to check nCount vs nFeature
FeatureScatter(
  seurat,
  feature1 = "nCount_RNA",
  feature2 = "nFeature_RNA",
  group.by = "orig.ident"    
) +
  ggtitle("nCount vs nFeature (before filtering)")

# Filtering cells to remove low genes and high mitochondrial %
seurat <- subset(seurat, subset = nFeature_RNA > 200 & percent.mt < 15) 

# Violion plot after filtering
VlnPlot(
  seurat,
  features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),
  group.by = "orig.ident",
  ncol     = 3,
  pt.size  = 0
) 

```

```{r}
# Normalizing data and finding variable features

# Setting seed for reproducibility
set.seed(42)
seurat <- subset(seurat, downsample = 8000)  # 8000 cells per sample = ~40,000 total

#rm(list = setdiff(ls(), "seurat")) 
#gc()
seurat <- NormalizeData(seurat, normalization.method = "LogNormalize", scale.factor = 10000)

seurat <- FindVariableFeatures(
  seurat,
  selection.method = "vst",
  nfeatures = 2000
)

# Plot top variable genes
top20 <- head(VariableFeatures(seurat), 20)
plot1 <- VariableFeaturePlot(seurat) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

LabelPoints(
  plot   = plot1,
  points = top20,
  repel  = TRUE,
  xnudge = 0,      # required when repel=TRUE
  ynudge = 0
) + ggtitle("Top 20 Highly Variable Features")

# scaling all genes 
seurat <- ScaleData(seurat, features = VariableFeatures(seurat))

```

```{r}
# PCA

seurat <- RunPCA(seurat, features = VariableFeatures(seurat))

# Elbow plot to decide how many PCs to use
ElbowPlot(seurat, ndims = 30) +
  ggtitle("Elbow Plot")


# Visualizing top genes driving PC1 and PC2
VizDimLoadings(seurat, dims = 1:2, reduction = "pca")

```

```{r}
# Clustering
seurat <- FindNeighbors(seurat, dims = 1:20)

seurat <- FindClusters(seurat, resolution = 0.5)

```

```{r}
# UMAP

seurat <- RunUMAP(seurat, dims = 1:20)

# UMAP coloured by cluster
p1 <- DimPlot(seurat, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +
  ggtitle("UMAP - Seurat Clusters")

# UMAP coloured by sample/timepoint to check batch effects
p2 <- DimPlot(seurat, reduction = "umap", group.by = "orig.ident") +
  ggtitle("UMAP - Sample Identity")

print(p1)
print(p2)

```

```{r}

# Automated Annotation with SingleR

# Converting seurat object
sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(seurat)

# Load mouse RNA-seq reference (ImmGen also works for immune-focused studies)
ref <- MouseRNAseqData()

# Running SingleR
pred <- SingleR(
  test   = sce,
  ref    = ref,
  labels = ref$label.main
)

# Inspecting predicted labels
print(table(pred$labels))

seurat$singler_labels <- pred$labels

# Heatmap
plotScoreHeatmap(pred)

# Delta distribution plot 
plotDeltaDistribution(pred, ncol = 3)

Idents(seurat) <- seurat$singler_labels

# UMAP coloured by annotated cell type
DimPlot(seurat, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +
  ggtitle("UMAP - SingleR Cell Type Annotation") +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

```{r}
# Finding cluster markers for manual verification

seurat_small <- subset(seurat, downsample = 500)

markers <- FindAllMarkers(
  seurat_small,
  only.pos = TRUE,
  min.pct = 0.25,
  logfc.threshold = 0.25
)

head(markers)

# Top 5 markers per cell type
top_markers <- markers %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = avg_log2FC, n = 5)

print(top_markers)

saveRDS(markers, "C:/Users/sharo/OneDrive/BINF 6110/Assignment_04/markers.rds")
write.csv(markers, "C:/Users/sharo/OneDrive/BINF 6110/Assignment_04/all_cluster_markers.csv", row.names = TRUE)


```


```{r}
# Feature plots

# T cells: Cd3d, Cd8a
# B cells: Ms4a1 (CD20)
# Macrophages/Monocytes: Lyz2, Csf1r
# Epithelial: Epcam, Krt8
# NK cells: Ncr1
# Dendritic cells: Itgax (CD11c)

  
FeaturePlot(
  seurat,
  features = c("Cd3d","Cd8a","Ms4a1","Ncr1"),
  ncol = 2,
  min.cutoff = "q5"
)

FeaturePlot(
  seurat,
  features = c("Lyz2","Itgax","Csf1r","Epcam"),
  ncol = 2,
  min.cutoff = "q5"
)

# Violin plots for top markers across annotated cell types
VlnPlot(
  seurat,
  features = c("Lyz2", "Cd3d", "Ms4a1", "Epcam"),
  ncol     = 2,
  pt.size  = 0
) & theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7, angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```

```{r}
# Cell composition analysis

# Counting cells per sample and cell type
comp <- seurat@meta.data %>%
  group_by(orig.ident, singler_labels) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

# Keeping top 10 most abundant cell types overall
top_types <- comp %>%
  group_by(singler_labels) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(n)) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = total, n = 10) %>%
  pull(singler_labels)

# Collapsing remaining rare cell types into "Other"
comp_plot <- comp %>%
  mutate(celltype_plot = ifelse(singler_labels %in% top_types,
                                singler_labels,
                                "Other")) %>%
  group_by(orig.ident, celltype_plot) %>%
  summarise(n = sum(n), .groups = "drop")

# Relative proportions

comp_prop <- comp_plot %>%
  group_by(orig.ident) %>%
  mutate(freq = n / sum(n))

p3 <- ggplot(comp_prop, aes(x = orig.ident, y = freq, fill = celltype_plot)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
  labs(
    title = "Cell Type Composition Per Sample",
    x = "Sample",
    y = "Proportion",
    fill = "Cell Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

p3

# Absolute cell counts
p4 <- ggplot(comp_plot, aes(x = orig.ident, y = n, fill = celltype_plot)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
  labs(
    title = "Absolute Cell Counts Per Sample",
    x = "Sample",
    y = "Cell Count",
    fill = "Cell Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

p4



```


```{r}

# Differential expression within macrophages (D02 vs D14)

# Subseting macrophages 
cluster_cells <- subset(seurat, idents = "Macrophages")

# Setting identity to sample for DE 
Idents(cluster_cells) <- cluster_cells$orig.ident

# D02 vs D14
# Comparing early vs late infection response
de_all <- FindMarkers(
  cluster_cells,
  ident.1         = "D02",
  ident.2         = "D14",
  min.pct         = 0.1,
  logfc.threshold = 0      
)

# Filtering for significant results 
de_sig <- de_all %>%
  filter(p_val_adj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_log2FC))

de_all
de_sig

write.csv(de_sig, "C:/Users/sharo/OneDrive/BINF 6110/Assignment_04/DE_results_macrophages.csv", row.names = TRUE)
write.csv(de_all, "C:/Users/sharo/OneDrive/BINF 6110/Assignment_04/DE_results_macrophages_all.csv", row.names = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# Volcano Plot
# visualizing effect size vs significance
EnhancedVolcano(
  de_all,
  lab            = rownames(de_all),
  x              = "avg_log2FC",
  y              = "p_val_adj",
  title          = "Macrophages: D02 vs D14",
  subtitle       = "Influenza A Virus nasal mucosa infection",
  pCutoff        = 0.05,
  FCcutoff       = 0.5,
  pointSize      = 1.5,
  labSize        = 3.5,
  col            = c("grey70", "steelblue", "orange", "firebrick"),
  legendLabels   = c("NS", "Log2FC > 0.5", "p-adj < 0.05", "Both"),
  drawConnectors = TRUE,
  selectLab      = head(rownames(de_all[order(de_all$p_val_adj), ]), 15), # top 15 labels
  max.overlaps   = 20,
  boxedLabels    = TRUE   
)
```



```{r}
# ORA on significant upregulated genes in D02 vs D14 

up_genes   <- rownames(de_sig %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0))   # up in D02
down_genes <- rownames(de_sig %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0))   # up in D14

# GO Biological Process ORA — upregulated in early infection (D02)
ego_up <- enrichGO(
  gene          = up_genes,
  OrgDb         = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType       = "SYMBOL",
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  readable      = TRUE
)

# GO Biological Process ORA — upregulated in late infection (D14)
ego_down <- enrichGO(
  gene          = down_genes,
  OrgDb         = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType       = "SYMBOL",
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  readable      = TRUE
)

# Dot plots
if (!is.null(ego_up) && nrow(ego_up) > 0) {
  print(dotplot(ego_up, showCategory = 15) +
    ggtitle("ORA: GO BP — Upregulated in D02 Macrophages"))
}

if (!is.null(ego_down) && nrow(ego_down) > 0) {
  print(dotplot(ego_down, showCategory = 15) +
    ggtitle("ORA: GO BP — Upregulated in D14 Macrophages"))
}

```

```{r}
# GSEA


# Building ranked gene list
gene_list <- de_all$avg_log2FC
names(gene_list) <- rownames(de_all)
gene_list <- sort(gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)

# Removing duplicate gene names if any
gene_list <- gene_list[!duplicated(names(gene_list))]

# Running GSEA on GO Biological Process terms
gsea_res <- gseGO(
  geneList      = gene_list,
  OrgDb         = org.Mm.eg.db,
  keyType       = "SYMBOL",
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  verbose       = FALSE,
  seed          = 42
)

if (!is.null(gsea_res) && nrow(gsea_res) > 0) {
  # Dot plot
  print(dotplot(gsea_res, showCategory = 15, split = ".sign") +
    facet_grid(. ~ .sign) +
    ggtitle("GSEA: GO BP — Macrophages D02 vs D14"))

  # Ridge plot: distribution of enrichment scores
  library(enrichplot)
  print(ridgeplot(gsea_res, showCategory = 10) +
    ggtitle("GSEA Ridge Plot — Top Pathways"))

  # GSEA plot for the top enriched term
  print(gseaplot2(gsea_res, geneSetID = 1, title = gsea_res$Description[1]))

} else {
  cat("No significant GSEA terms found at p-adj < 0.05\n")
}

# Saving results
if (!is.null(gsea_res)) {
  write.csv(as.data.frame(gsea_res), "C:/Users/sharo/OneDrive/BINF 6110/Assignment_04/GSEA_results_macrophages.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}
```

```{r}
# Heatmap of top 20 DE genes in macrophages 
top_de_genes <- c(
  head(rownames(de_sig %>% filter(avg_log2FC > 0)), 10),   # top 10 up in D02
  head(rownames(de_sig %>% filter(avg_log2FC < 0)), 10)    # top 10 up in D14
)

# Only keeping genes that exist in the object
top_de_genes <- top_de_genes[top_de_genes %in% rownames(seurat)]

if (length(top_de_genes) > 0) {
  DoHeatmap(
    cluster_cells,
    features = top_de_genes,
    group.by = "orig.ident",
    size     = 3
  ) +
    ggtitle("Top DE Genes in Macrophages: D02 vs D14")
}

top_de_genes

# Combining into one final table
top_de_table <- bind_rows(
  de_sig %>%
    filter(avg_log2FC > 0) %>%
    arrange(p_val_adj) %>%
    head(10) %>%
    mutate(direction = "Up in D02"),
  
  de_sig %>%
    filter(avg_log2FC < 0) %>%
    arrange(p_val_adj) %>%
    head(10) %>%
    mutate(direction = "Up in D14")
) %>%
  dplyr::select(direction, avg_log2FC, pct.1, pct.2, p_val_adj) %>%
  mutate(
    avg_log2FC = round(avg_log2FC, 3),
    p_val_adj  = formatC(p_val_adj, format = "e", digits = 2)  # scientific notation
  )
top_de_table

write.csv(
  top_de_table,
  "C:/Users/sharo/OneDrive/BINF 6110/Assignment_04/top_DE_genes_macrophages.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)


```
